titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Sarzanensis (pars altera)
VL22A
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

listBibl.bibl: G. Godu, Codex Sarzanensis. Fragments d'ancienne version latine du quatrième Evangile (Spicilegium Casinense 2), Monte Cassino, 1936
G. Amelli, Un antichissimo codice biblico latino purpureo conservato nella chiesa di Sarezzano presso Tortona. Dissertazione critico-storica con note illustrative, Milan, 1872

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made in 2008 by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 65r-72v, based on Godu's edition. There are no Amelli readings in folios 58-72. This manuscript covers John 18:36-19:17and John 19:31-20:14and was bound with VL 22 in antiquity.The transcription is based on Godu's edition, distinguishing between letters still visible and letters no longer visible. It includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. We only provide reconstructed text for a line in which there is at least one letter extant, on the grounds that it is misleading to provide text for several or more lines which are completely missing. An exception is that we reconstruct partially extant words, and where a line begins and ends with such a word, text is supplied between them.The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: This concatenation has been removed, because if it is kept, the running title of 67v does not display
There is no running title on folio 70r or the subsequent pages
The 'um' of the correction of 'eor' to 'eorum' is visible above the line.
Godu reconstructs this as 'do¬m', but this form of nomen sacrum is not found elsewhere in this quaternion (cf. folio 71v)
The manuscript breaks off at this point.

seg.note: The quire signature in the bottom margin is preceded by a decorative device.
